title: Execute a Python script
summary: Execute a Python script. Stdout is captured line-by-line as Progress events and written to run.log. Stderr is captured line-by-line as Warning events and written to stderr.log. A non-zero exit code produces Finished { success: false }. Use the 'executable' field to select a virtualenv interpreter or a specific Python version.
description:
Runs a Python script and streams its output back to the Open Choice protocol. Provide a path to a `.py` file via `script`. Positional and named arguments can be forwarded to the script via `args` and `kwargs`. All output files (run.log, stderr.log) are written to `output_dir`.

The `executable` field selects the Python interpreter: a bare name resolved via PATH (`python`, `python3`, `python3.11`), a relative path to a virtualenv interpreter (`.venv/Scripts/python.exe` on Windows), or an absolute path. Using a virtualenv executable automatically activates that environment's site-packages.

The `trust_acknowledged` field must be set to `true`. This is an explicit safety gate: the plugin executes arbitrary Python code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process.
usage:
[["python-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "<path/to/script.py>"
output_dir = "<dir>"
trust_acknowledged = true
# optional:
args = ["arg1", "arg2"]
kwargs = { key = "value" }
executable = ".venv/Scripts/python.exe"

arguments.name: script
arguments.description: Path to the .py file to execute. Relative paths are resolved from the directory containing the .oce file. Absolute paths are used as-is.

arguments.name: args
arguments.description: Positional arguments appended to the python command after the script path. Accessed in Python via sys.argv[1:].

arguments.name: kwargs
arguments.description: Named arguments passed as --key value pairs after positional args. Accessed in Python via sys.argv or an arg-parser library such as argparse.

arguments.name: executable
arguments.description: Python executable to invoke. Defaults to 'python' when omitted. Accepts: (1) a bare name resolved via PATH ('python3', 'python3.11'); (2) a relative path to a virtualenv binary ('.venv/Scripts/python.exe' on Windows, '.venv/bin/python' on macOS/Linux) — relative paths are resolved from the .oce file directory; (3) an absolute path.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory where run.log and stderr.log are written. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: trust_acknowledged
arguments.description: Must be true. Explicitly acknowledges that this plugin executes arbitrary Python code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process, and that the script source is trusted.

details:
`run_script` is a thin execution wrapper: it invokes the Python interpreter on the provided script, streams stdout line-by-line as Progress events, and writes all output to `output_dir/run.log`. Stderr lines are captured as Warning events and written to `output_dir/stderr.log`.

The `trust_acknowledged = true` field is required and intentional — it prevents accidental execution of untrusted code by requiring the caller to explicitly opt in.

Argument forwarding:
- `args` becomes positional arguments after the script path on the command line
- `kwargs` becomes `--key value` pairs following the positional args
In Python, retrieve them with `sys.argv[1:]`. For structured parsing, use `argparse` from the standard library.

For virtualenv usage, point `executable` at the venv's `python` binary — this activates that environment's site-packages automatically without needing to source `activate`.
value:
On success, `output_dir` contains:

- `run.log` — all stdout lines from the Python process.
- `stderr.log` — all stderr lines from the Python process.

The plugin emits `event.run.started`, per-line Progress events, and `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed` on non-zero exit).
notes: The `trust_acknowledged` field must be `true`; any other value (including omitting it) causes the command to reject immediately.
Non-zero exit codes from Python produce `event.run.failed`; the log files are still written.
Relative `executable` paths are resolved from the .oce file's directory, not the working directory.
The virtualenv must already exist and have its packages installed — the wrapper does not create or install into the environment.

see_also.description: R equivalent — run a .R file or inline R code.
Julia equivalent — run a .jl file and optionally activate a Julia project environment.

examples.title: Run a Python script with the system interpreter
Run a script inside a virtualenv
Pass kwargs to the script using argparse
examples.body:
[["python-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/export.py"
kwargs = { input = "./data/part_worths.csv", output = "./outputs/summary.csv", n_top = "10" }
output_dir = "./runs/py-export"
trust_acknowledged = true

examples.title: Run with a specific Python version
examples.body:
[["python-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/simulate.py"
executable = "python3.11"
args = ["1000"]
output_dir = "./runs/py-simulate"
trust_acknowledged = true

fields.name: script
fields.description: Path to the .py file to execute.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Positional args forwarded to the script via sys.argv.

fields.name: kwargs
fields.description: Named args forwarded as --key value pairs.

fields.name: executable
fields.description: Python executable. Default: python. Accepts PATH name, venv path, or absolute path.

fields.name: trust_acknowledged
fields.description: Must be true. Safety gate for arbitrary code execution.

artifacts.title: Script stdout log
artifacts.description: Every line written to stdout by the script, captured in order. Review this to see printed results, progress messages, and any output the script produced.

artifacts.title: Script stderr log
artifacts.description: Every line written to stderr by the script, captured as warnings. Review this first if the run fails — error messages and stack traces appear here.
